name: Heirloom tomatoes
why_now: July is peak tomato season in Southern California. Any ripe tomato — heirloom or not — is at its best right now. The heat concentrates sugars across every variety. Heirlooms are worth it if you see good ones, but a ripe vine tomato from Trader Joe's or Whole Foods will do the same work.
how_to_choose: Irregular shape is a good sign, not a flaw
Avoid anything refrigerated at the store
buy_this_much: 4–6 medium tomatoes for one person for the week

flavor_paths.name: Italian Summer
flavor_paths.description: Olive oil, basil, garlic, black pepper, flaky salt.

flavor_paths.name: Bright Taco
flavor_paths.description: Lime, cilantro, corn, cucumber, a little heat.

flavor_paths.name: Simple Toast
flavor_paths.description: Sourdough, olive oil, salt. The tomato does the rest.

weekday_uses: Pasta sauce with garlic and olive oil
Toast with salt and olive oil
weekend_use: Thick slices with burrata or fresh mozzarella, basil, olive oil, and sourdough for dragging. Burrata is richer; fresh mozzarella is half the price and nearly as good.
storage: Counter only. Never refrigerate. Use within four or five days.
one_thing_to_learn: Smell the stem end before you buy. A tomato that smells like a tomato at the store will taste like one at home.
market_question: Which variety is ripest right now?
